The hairy xeranthemum has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing pain and fighting off infections. The bioactive constituents of Xeranthemum pilosum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
